svg.style: background: transparent; background-color: transparent; color-scheme: dark;

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 486px; height: 1px; padding-top: 20px; margin-left: 19px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #1b2733;
display: inline-block; font-size: 13px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#1b2733, #c5cfda); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
div.font: The app you ship. You own the screens (plugin gallery, runner, settings), the approval bottom sheets the user sees, and the theming.

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(221, 244, 255), rgb(14, 34, 43)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(9, 105, 218), rgb(86, 168, 255));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 486px; height: 1px; padding-top: 195px; margin-left: 19px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #1b2733;
display: inline-block; font-size: 13px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#1b2733, #c5cfda); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
div.font: The mobile adapter. Ships a WebView with injected JS that intercepts fetch / XMLHttpRequest, a headless NativeProver wrapping the tlsn-native Expo module (a native Rust port of the prover — Hermes can't run the WASM build), and a PluginRenderer that maps the plugin's DomJson tree onto React Native primitives.

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(243, 245, 248), rgb(25, 27, 29)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(36, 63, 95), rgb(166, 189, 217));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 486px; height: 1px; padding-top: 392px; margin-left: 19px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #1b2733;
display: inline-block; font-size: 13px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#1b2733, #c5cfda); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
div.font: The protocol core. HostCore drives the plugin lifecycle — it evaluates the plugin's JavaScript in a sandbox, runs the reactive main() loop, and exposes the capabilities a plugin uses (prove(), openWindow(), useHeaders(), …). Owns every protocol type the plugin and host exchange, and knows nothing about whether it runs on a phone, a browser, or a Node CLI.
